summary: Goat Funded Futures scores 65/100 overall. You get 100% of the first $10K in profit, and can choose between subscription-based or one-time fee models. All plans include access to 55 futures markets and advanced platforms like NinjaTrader, Quantower, and Project X, with no added costs for data or software. The futures prop firm has clear payout rules and flexible account types, but weak customer support and high reset fees limit its appeal.
pros: Monthly subscription or one-time fee options
cons: No resets for Instant Funding accounts
Profit consistency rules on all models
bestFor: Experienced futures traders who want flexible evaluation models, access to advanced platforms like NinjaTrader and Quantower, and the ability to keep 100% of their first $10K in profit
notIdealFor: Beginners who need structured education, traders who rely on active support, or those who want reset options on Instant Funding accounts

categorySections.challenges: GFF offers three evaluation types: EOD, Static, and Instant Funding. EOD uses monthly subscription ($99–$249/mo) with trailing EOD drawdown and mid-phase scaling. Static uses monthly subscription ($199–$349/mo) with fixed drawdown and no daily loss limits. Instant is a one-time fee ($349–$699) with trailing real-time drawdown and 3% daily cap. Consistency rules apply across all models. No resets for Instant. Scored 7/10.
categorySections.spreads: Raw spread access to CME-traded instruments with no data, platform, or exchange surcharges. Per-contract commissions are not publicly disclosed but typically range $1.50–$2.50 per round turn. No differentiation across asset classes. Transparent fee structure but lacks exact commission rate disclosure. Scored 6/10.
categorySections.markets: Access to 55 tradable instruments across CME Group markets including equity indices (ES, NQ, YM + micros), metals (GC, SI + micros), energy (CL, MCL, NG), currency futures (6E, 6B, 6J), and agricultural/soft commodities (ZC, ZS, KC). Both full-size and micro contracts supported. Variable margin aligned with CME practices. Scored 6/10.
categorySections.platforms: Full suite of futures platforms with no extra fees: Project X (proprietary), NinjaTrader, Quantower, Volumetrica, Volsys, ATAS, TradingView, VolBook, and Tradovate. Real-time data included. Copy trading between own accounts allowed. Algorithmic trading support not confirmed. Scored 8/10.
categorySections.payouts: 100% of first $10K profit kept, then 90% split scaling to 92% and 95% at GOAT Trader level. EOD/Static pay every 14 days, Instant every 10 days. Min 6 trading days per cycle. Fixed payout windows (5th–8th and 20th–23rd). Min withdrawal $500 (EOD/Static), $250 (Instant). Processed via Riseworks in 2–3 business days. Scored 7/10.
categorySections.support: 24/7 support via email, live chat, and Discord. Response times are slow with vague answers reported. Many traders report delays and unhelpful responses on Trustpilot. Discord support staff not reliably active. No tutorials, webinars, or platform walkthroughs. Only basic FAQ available.
categorySections.trust: 3.8/5 Trustpilot from 120+ reviews. Not broker-backed. ~30% negative reviews citing account credential delays, poor follow-up, and unresolved escalations. Discord moderation complaints. Limited payout transparency — verified success stories are rare. Scored 5/10.

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict: 65/100
verdictBlocks.body:
Goat Funded Futures scored 65/100 in our testing. The biggest advantage is the ability to keep 100% of your first $10K in profit, paired with access to raw spreads, advanced futures platforms, and Instant Funding for those who want to skip evaluations. You can choose between flexible subscription models or a one-time fee, and funded accounts benefit from a well-structured scaling plan. Payouts are fast, transparent, and handled via Riseworks.

That said, the firm still has key weaknesses. There's no reset option for Instant accounts, education is limited, and support responses are often slow or vague. GFF also lacks transparency on fees like commissions and leverage, which could make planning harder if you're not familiar with futures. If you already understand CME trading and want fast access to funding, GFF is worth a look. For beginners or traders who value active support, other options may offer a smoother experience.

faq.q: What is the profit split on Goat Funded Futures?
faq.a: You keep 100% of your first $10,000 in profit. After that, the split starts at 90% and can increase to 92% and eventually 95% at the GOAT Trader level. This applies across all account types — EOD, Static, and Instant Funding.

faq.q: Does Goat Funded Futures offer Instant Funding?
faq.a: Yes. The Instant Funding model requires a one-time fee ($349–$699 depending on account size) and gives you immediate access to a funded account. You need to hit a 7% profit target and complete at least 6 trades over 10 days to qualify for your first payout. There's no reset option if you breach the rules.

faq.q: What platforms does Goat Funded Futures support?
faq.a: GFF supports a full suite of futures platforms including their proprietary Project X, NinjaTrader, Quantower, Volumetrica, Volsys, ATAS, TradingView, VolBook, and Tradovate. All platforms come with real-time data and no additional fees.

faq.q: Can you reset an Instant Funding account at GFF?
faq.a: No. The Instant Funding model does not allow resets. If you breach a rule, you'll need to purchase a new plan. EOD accounts can be reset for $99 and Static accounts for $349.
